mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a robust and expanding economy. This leads to higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS value of 152383.0 indicates high employment level
failure_condition: A positive 3-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the headline inflation index (CPIAUCSL) rises above a key psychological or policy threshold, it forces the central bank to tighten monetary policy aggressively. The resulting increase in discount rates and recession risk causes negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest CPIAUCSL value is 258.501, above the trigger threshold
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL is above 250, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in a statistically significant number of cases.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a very low unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a late-cycle economy where the central bank is actively trying to curb inflation. This 'dual-pressure' scenario increases the risk of a policy-induced slowdown, negatively impacting equities.
failure_condition: The model's signal has a negative rank information coefficient over a rolling 3-year window, indicating no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, a negative surprise or decelerating trend in retail sales (RSAFS) is a primary indicator of weakening consumer demand, which directly pressures corporate revenues and leads to negative forward returns as earnings estimates are revised down.
failure_condition: Instances of weak retail sales growth are not followed by statistically significant negative S&P 500 returns relative to the baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a downturn in the manufacturing and goods-producing sectors of the economy. This is a leading indicator of broader economic weakness and recession, causing a negative repricing of equities.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to produce negative 60-day returns in over 60% of instances where the INDPRO trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Strength in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer balance sheets and confidence, with positive spillover effects into related sectors. This provides a short-term boost to market sentiment and positive returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between monthly changes in HOUST and subsequent 5-day S&P 500 returns is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) is slowing while the central bank is raising the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), the market prices in a high probability of a policy error (over-tightening) that will cause a recession. This dynamic leads to sharply negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 6-month change > 0 and GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter change < prior quarter change
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is 1.55%, suggesting a prior hiking trend
failure_condition: During periods matching the trigger condition, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE), even from a low base, is one of the most reliable leading indicators of recession. As the rate ticks up, markets price in lower future growth and earnings, causing negative equity performance.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average is greater than 12-month moving average (Sahm Rule variant)
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE is at a cyclical low of 3.5%
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate for predicting negative 20-day returns is below 55% over a full business cycle.
